summary: End-to-end testing of Recolecta (Uber-for-garbage) — 20/20 backend pytest tests passed; full UI verification for Landing, Login (OTP mock), Resident, Driver and Admin dashboards passed with no JS errors.

minor.issue: pytest-xdist (configured in /app/backend/pytest.ini with -n auto) caused a race on the shared db.otps collection when multiple workers exercise the same seeded phone — tests still pass when run with `-o addopts=''` (single-process). Not an app bug, but the OTP overwrite-on-request semantics (per-phone single code) is inherently race-prone for parallel automation.

issues: Hero text 'UBER PARA LA BASURA' is split across two lines with 'BASURA' inside an orange highlight box — visually correct and matches the requested aesthetic.
Map fallback explicitly labelled 'MAPA SIMULADO · FALTA GOOGLE MAPS KEY' which is expected per agent_to_agent_context_note.

action_items: (Optional) Consider switching pytest.ini default from `-n auto` to single-process for OTP-based suites, OR namespace test phones per worker. Not required — testing already works with -o addopts=''.
(Optional) The TestSpecialPickups.test_resident_create_pickup_and_notification function ends with `return sp['id']` which triggers a PytestReturnNotNoneWarning. Replace with `assert` for cleanliness.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is one ~560-line monolith. As routes grow, consider splitting into routers (auth, trucks, routes, special_pickups, admin) for maintainability.
All endpoints correctly project `_id: 0` away from Mongo results — no ObjectId leakage observed.
JWT secret falls back to a hard-coded default if JWT_SECRET env var is unset. Acceptable for demo but should be required in prod.

OTP storage uses upsert keyed on phone, so a fresh request-otp overwrites any pending code; for production this is fine (per-phone latest code wins) but for parallel test execution it causes races (see backend_issues.minor).
Pydantic models use Field(default_factory=uuid.uuid4) and ConfigDict(extra='ignore') — good.
require_role correctly returns 403 (verified: resident & driver both get 403 on /api/admin/stats).
ResidentDashboard's edit-address modal inputs do not have data-testid attributes (address/lat/lng fields), unlike the create-pickup modal which does. Low-priority test-friendliness gap.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of requested flows (landing, OTP login for 3 roles, resident ETA + map + special pickup + edit-address modal, driver toggle-sharing + mark-collected, admin stats + schedule-pickup + create-truck)

test_credentials: 5550000001 admin, 5550000002 driver (Juan, truck CDMX-001 on_route), 5550000004 resident (Carlos, has lat/lng). OTP mock_code returned in /api/auth/request-otp body and rendered via [data-testid=login-mock-code].
seed_data_creation: Tests created several `TEST_`-prefixed special pickups and trucks (TEST-UI99, TEST-XXXXX); not auto-cleaned. Not harmful, but admin stats count will reflect them.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run backend tests with `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_recolecta_api.py -v -o addopts='' --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/recolecta_results.xml`. The pytest.ini default uses `-n auto` which causes OTP races — override addopts to run single-process. Resident phone 5550000004 is seeded with address near CDMX center and truck CDMX-001 is seeded as `on_route` so /api/resident/eta returns available:true immediately. Google Maps key is intentionally empty — the simulated fallback (data-testid='map-view' with truck/home divs) IS the expected behavior.
rca of the issue: No application bugs found. Only a test-environment race in pytest-xdist (multiple workers calling request-otp for the same seeded phone overwrite the OTP row before the other worker verifies). Mitigation already applied in our run by overriding addopts to disable xdist.
